summary: Recent changes to the Firefox browser have focused on improving how audio sessions are managed during web browsing. This includes the addition of a dedicated system for tracking audio session states, which helps ensure that audio from different tabs behaves correctly when users interact with the browser. In particular, developers implemented algorithms that facilitate smooth transitions between different audio states, making it easier to manage background audio and improve user experience. These enhancements ultimately support better audio handling, particularly for applications that rely on features like _Web Audio_ and _Media Source Extensions_ for streaming content. As a result, users can expect **more reliable and consistent audio behavior across various websites**, especially when switching between tabs or using the picture-in-picture mode to watch videos.
